title: Performance, Migration, API Design, and Release Evidence · TypeScript 7 Performance Board
summary: Solve unfamiliar TypeScript 7 inference, narrowing, generic, module, configuration, runtime-validation, async, testing, migration, and release incidents without workshop solution shapes. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish compile-time type evidence from emitted JavaScript and runtime validation.
Record compiler, runtime, configuration, dependency, and module conditions needed to reproduce a type result.
Use satisfies to check a value against a contract while retaining useful inferred literal information.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run
steps.instruction: Order the Static Runtime Boundary evidence cycle for this exam task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Static Runtime Boundary assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A exam build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 2.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Static Runtime Boundary has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Static Runtime Boundary precondition or invariant that the exam case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A exam build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 2.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A passing type check proves every runtime input is valid.

options.text: Declare the domain relationship and identify every untrusted runtime boundary
Infer or annotate the smallest sound type and validate unknown values before narrowing
Run strict diagnostics, inspect emitted JavaScript, and execute the changed case
Add compile-time rejection and runtime boundary evidence, then document remaining limits
